title: Electrical and Electronics Drafters
description: Prepare wiring diagrams, circuit board assembly diagrams, and layout drawings used for the manufacture, installation, or repair of electrical equipment.

tasks.text: Draft detail and assembly drawings of design components, circuitry or printed circuit boards, using computer-assisted equipment or standard drafting techniques and devices.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Draft working drawings, wiring diagrams, wiring connection specifications, or cross-sections of underground cables, as required for instructions to installation crew.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assemble documentation packages and produce drawing sets to be checked by an engineer or an architect.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review completed construction drawings and cost estimates for accuracy and conformity to standards and regulations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Consult with engineers to discuss or interpret design concepts, or determine requirements of detailed working drawings.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Confer with engineering staff and other personnel to resolve problems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Measure factors that affect installation and arrangement of equipment, such as distances to be spanned by wire and cable.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Design electrical systems, such as lighting systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Draw master sketches to scale showing relation of proposed installations to existing facilities and exact specifications and dimensions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review work orders or procedural manuals and confer with vendors or design staff to resolve problems or modify design.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Locate files relating to specified design project in database library, load program into computer, and record completed job data.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine electronic schematics and supporting documents to develop, compute, and verify specifications for drafting data, such as configuration of parts, dimensions, or tolerances.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Compare logic element configuration on display screen with engineering schematics and calculate figures to convert, redesign, or modify element.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review blueprints to determine customer requirements and consult with assembler regarding schematics, wiring procedures, or conductor paths.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Study work order requests to determine type of service, such as lighting or power, demanded by installation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Explain drawings to production or construction teams and provide adjustments, as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Reproduce working drawings on copy machines or trace drawings in ink.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Generate computer tapes of final layout design to produce layered photo masks or photo plotting design onto film.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Determine the order of work and the method of presentation, such as orthographic or isometric drawing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Visit proposed installation sites and draw rough sketches of location.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Key and program specified commands and engineering specifications into computer system to change functions and test final layout.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Copy drawings of printed circuit board fabrication using print machine or blueprinting procedure.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select drill size to drill test head, according to test design and specifications, and submit guide layout to designated department.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Plot electrical test points on layout sheets and draw schematics for wiring test fixture heads to frames.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Write technical reports and draw charts that display statistics and data.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Supervise and coordinate work activities of workers engaged in drafting, designing layouts, assembling, or testing printed circuit boards.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Train students to use drafting machines and to prepare schematic diagrams, block diagrams, control drawings, logic diagrams, integrated circuit drawings, or interconnection diagrams.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare and interpret specifications, calculating weights, volumes, or stress factors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Supervise or train other technologists, technicians, or drafters.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Use computer-aided drafting equipment or conventional drafting stations, technical handbooks, tables, calculators, or traditional drafting tools, such as boards, pencils, protractors, or T-squares.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
